test_name: 105. Mass Strandings of Whales and Dolphins

content.cn_text: 第四部分。
content.en_text: Section 4.

content.cn_text: 你将听到一段讲座，内容是关于某些海洋生物行为相关的问题。
content.en_text: You will hear part of a lecture about a problem related to the behaviour of certain sea creatures.

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第31至第40题。
content.en_text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第31至第40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 大家下午好。
content.en_text: Good afternoon everyone.

content.cn_text: 你们中有些人即将外出做实地工作，所以今天下午
content.en_text: Well with some of you about to go out on field work, it's timely that in this afternoon

content.cn_text: 这一节我会分享一些观点，解释为什么成群的鲸鱼和海豚
content.en_text: session I'll be sharing some ideas about the reasons why groups of whales and dolphins

content.cn_text: 有时会从海里游到岸边，直接搁浅在海滩上，并且通常会死亡，
content.en_text: sometimes swim ashore from the sea right onto the beach and, most often, die in what

content.cn_text: 这种现象被称为集体搁浅。
content.en_text: are known as mass strandings.

content.cn_text: 不幸的是，这类事件在你们将要前往的一些地点经常发生，
content.en_text: Unfortunately, this type of event is a frequent occurrence in some of the locations that

content.cn_text: 在那里，有时潮水会突然退去，使动物感到困惑。
content.en_text: you'll be travelling to, where sometimes the tide goes out suddenly, confusing the animals.

content.cn_text: 不过，关于集体搁浅的原因还有许多其他理论。
content.en_text: However, there are many other theories about the causes of mass strandings.

content.cn_text: 第一个理论认为，这种行为与寄生虫有关。
content.en_text: The first is that the behaviour is linked to parasites.

content.cn_text: 人们经常发现，搁浅动物体内有大量寄生虫。
content.en_text: It's often found that stranded animals were infested with large numbers of parasites.

content.cn_text: 例如，在死亡鲸鱼的耳朵里常常发现一种蠕虫。
content.en_text: For instance, a type of worm is commonly found in the ears of dead whales.

content.cn_text: 由于海洋动物高度依赖听觉来导航，这种寄生虫感染
content.en_text: Since marine animals rely heavily on their hearing to navigate, this type of infestation

content.cn_text: 可能非常有害。
content.en_text: has the potential to be very harmful.

content.cn_text: 另一个理论与毒素或毒物有关。
content.en_text: Another theory is related to toxins or poisons.

content.cn_text: 研究也发现，这些物质会导致许多海洋动物死亡。
content.en_text: These have also been found to contribute to the death of many marine animals.

content.cn_text: 我相信你们知道，许多毒素来自植物或动物。
content.en_text: Many toxins, as I'm sure you're aware, originate from plants or animals.

content.cn_text: 鲸鱼在正常进食行为中摄入这些毒素，但这些毒物
content.en_text: The whale ingests these toxins in its normal feeding behaviour, but whether these poisons

content.cn_text: 是直接还是间接导致搁浅和死亡，似乎取决于所涉及的毒素。
content.en_text: directly or indirectly lead to stranding and death, seems to depend upon the toxin involved.

content.cn_text: 例如，1988年，在 Cape Cod 海滩搁浅后被检查的14头座头鲸，
content.en_text: In 1988, for example, 14 humpback whales examined after stranding along the beaches of Cape

content.cn_text: 被发现是在吃了含有石房蛤毒素的金枪鱼后中毒，
content.en_text: Cod were found to have been poisoned after eating tuna that contained saxitoxin,

content.cn_text: 这种毒素对人类也可能致命。
content.en_text: the same toxin that can be fatal in humans.

content.cn_text: 另一种说法认为，一些动物是在追逐过程中跟随
content.en_text: Alternatively, it has also been suggested that some animals strand accidentally by following

content.cn_text: 猎物上岸，从而意外搁浅。
content.en_text: their prey ashore in the confusion of the chase.

content.cn_text: 1995年，David Thurston 监测到领航鲸在追随鱿鱼上岸后搁浅。
content.en_text: In 1995, David Thurston monitored pilot whales that beached after following squid ashore.

content.cn_text: 然而，这一观点似乎并不适用于大多数集体搁浅，
content.en_text: However, this idea does not seem to hold true for the majority of mass strandings,

content.cn_text: 因为对这些动物胃内容物的检查显示，大多数动物搁浅时并没有进食，
content.en_text: because examination of the animal's stomach contents revealed that most had not been feeding

content.cn_text: 它们搁浅时并未进食。还有一些新理论将搁浅与人类联系起来。
content.en_text: as they stranded. There are also some new theories which link strandings to humans.

content.cn_text: 一个越来越令人担忧的问题是，海洋中的巨大噪音会导致搁浅。
content.en_text: A growing concern is that loud noises in the ocean cause strandings.

content.cn_text: 军事演习造成的噪音尤其令人担忧，
content.en_text: Noises such as those caused by military exercises are of particular concern,

content.cn_text: 并且最近已被指出是一些搁浅事件的原因。
content.en_text: and have been pinpointed as the cause of some strandings of late.

content.cn_text: 其中一个例子是2000年巴哈马发生的鲸鱼集体搁浅，
content.en_text: One of these, a mass stranding of whales in 2000 in the Bahamas,

content.cn_text: 它与使用一种新型潜艇探测系统的实验时间非常接近。
content.en_text: coincided closely with experiments using a new submarine detection system.

content.cn_text: 有几个因素使这次搁浅显得不同于以往的搁浅事件。
content.en_text: There were several factors that made this stranding stand out as different from previous strandings.

content.cn_text: 这促使研究人员寻找新的原因。
content.en_text: This led researchers to look for a new cause.

content.cn_text: 首先，所有搁浅动物都是健康的。
content.en_text: For one, all the stranded animals were healthy.

content.cn_text: 此外，这些动物分散在38公里长的海岸线上，
content.en_text: In addition, the animals were spread out along 38 kilometers of coast,

content.cn_text: 而在集体搁浅发生时，更常见的是这些动物被发现聚在一起。
content.en_text: whereas it's more common for the animals to be found in a group when mass strandings occur.

content.cn_text: 最后一个理论与群体行为有关，
content.en_text: A final theory is related to group behavior,

content.cn_text: 认为海洋哺乳动物无法区分生病和健康的领头者，
content.en_text: and suggests that sea mammals cannot distinguish between sick and healthy leaders,

content.cn_text: 会跟随生病的领头者，即使走向不可避免的死亡。
content.en_text: and will follow sick leaders, even to an inevitable death.

content.cn_text: 这是一个特别有意思的理论，因为被认为最具社会性的鲸类，
content.en_text: This is a particularly interesting theory, since the whales that are thought to be most social,

content.cn_text: 也就是齿鲸，是最频繁搁浅的群体。
content.en_text: the toothed whales, are the group that strand the most frequently.

content.cn_text: 这一理论也得到了1994年一次海豚搁浅证据的支持。
content.en_text: The theory is also supported by evidence from a dolphin stranding in 1994.

content.cn_text: 对死亡动物的检查显示，除了领头者之外，
content.en_text: Examination of the dead animals revealed that, apart from the leader,

content.cn_text: 其他所有海豚在死亡时都是健康的。
content.en_text: all the others had been healthy at the time of their death.

content.cn_text: 然而，如果没有一个一致的理论，我们很难对这一
content.en_text: Without one consistent theory, however, it is very hard for us to do anything about this

content.cn_text: 现象采取任何措施，只能在可能的时候帮助这些动物。
content.en_text: phenomenon, except to assist animals where and when we can.

content.cn_text: 世界各地已经建立了搁浅救援网络，
content.en_text: Stranding networks have been established around the world,

content.cn_text: 用于帮助救援动物，并从无法救助的动物身上采集样本。
content.en_text: to aid in rescuing animals and collecting samples from those that could not be helped.

content.cn_text: 我推荐 John Connor 的《Marine Mammals Ashore》，
content.en_text: I recommend John Connor's Marine Mammals Ashore,

content.cn_text: 如果你有兴趣进一步了解这些网络，或者自己建立一个网络，
content.en_text: as an excellent starting point if you're interested in finding out more about these networks,

content.cn_text: 这本书是一个很好的起点。
content.en_text: or establishing one yourself.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 4.

content.cn_text: 现在你有半分钟检查答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

content.cn_text: 听力测试到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of the listening test.

content.cn_text: 在雅思考试中，你现在会有10分钟把答案誊写到答题纸上。
content.en_text: In the IELTS test, you would now have 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the answer sheet.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: the 31. __________ can change quickly.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问在哪些地区集体搁浅常见，即什么会快速变化。原文说 the tide goes out suddenly，所以答案是 tide。

items.title: Parasites: e.g. some parasites can affect marine animals' 32. __________, which they depend on for navigation
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问一些寄生虫会影响海洋动物依赖导航的什么。原文说寄生虫在 ears 中，并且 animals rely on their hearing to navigate，所以答案可以是 ears/hearing。

items.title: Toxins: Poisons from 33. __________ and animals, which are commonly consumed by whales e.g. Cape Cod (1988) – whales were killed by saxitoxin
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问毒物来自什么和动物。原文说 toxins originate from plants or animals，所以答案是 plants。

items.title: The majority of animals were not 34. __________ when they stranded
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问大多数动物搁浅时并没有在做什么。原文说 most had not been feeding as they stranded，所以答案是 feeding。

items.title: 35. __________ from military tests are linked to some recent strandings
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问军事测试中的什么与近期一些搁浅有关。原文说 loud noises，尤其是 military exercises caused noises，所以答案是 noises。

items.title: were all 36. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问2000年巴哈马搁浅事件中鲸鱼都是什么状态。原文说 all the stranded animals were healthy，所以答案是 healthy。

items.title: were not in a 37. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问巴哈马搁浅事件中鲸鱼并不是以什么形式出现。原文说常见情况是 animals found in a group，但这次是 spread out，所以答案是 group。

items.title: More strandings in the most 38. __________ species of whales
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问哪类鲸物种搁浅更多。原文说 the whales thought to be most social strand the most frequently，所以答案是 social。

items.title: 1994 dolphin stranding – only the 39. __________ was ill
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问1994年海豚搁浅中，只有谁生病了。原文说 apart from the leader, all the others had been healthy，所以答案是 leader。

items.title: Marine Mammals Ashore (Connor) gives information about stranding 40. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问《Marine Mammals Ashore》提供关于搁浅什么的信息。原文说 finding out more about these networks，所以答案是 networks。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
Mass Strandings of Whales and Dolphins Mass strandings:
• situations where groups of whales, dolphins, etc. swim onto the beach and die
Common in areas where:
• the $31$ can change quickly.
Several other theories:
• Parasites: e.g. some parasites can affect marine animals' $32$, which they depend on for navigation
• Toxins: Poisons from $33$ and animals, which are commonly consumed by whales e.g. Cape Cod (1988) – whales were killed by saxitoxin
Accidental Strandings
• Animals may follow prey ashore, e.g. Thurston (1995)
• The majority of animals were not $34$ when they stranded
Human Activity
• $35$ from military tests are linked to some recent strandings
• The Bahamas (2000) stranding was unusual because the whales
• were all $36$
• were not in a $37$
Group Behavior
• More strandings in the most $38$ species of whales
• 1994 dolphin stranding – only the $39$ was ill
Further Reading:
• Marine Mammals Ashore (Connor) gives information about stranding $40$.
